Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 015 [SB Gilgameš 08][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[mim-mu]-⸢u₂⸣ še-e-ri a-na na₂-ma-ri dGIŠ-⸢gim₂⸣-[maš ...]

At the first glimmer of dawn, Gilgameš [...]:

o 22

[...] ⸢AMA⸣-ka MAŠ.DA₃ a-ka-a-nu a-bu-ka ⸢ib⸣-[...]

'[...] your mother, a gazelle, your father, a wild donkey, ... [...];

o 33

[... i]-⸢na⸣ še-zib-bi-šin u₂!(I-)ra-bu-u-ka ka-a-⸢šu⸣ [...]

'[...] reared with their milk, [...]

o 44

[u₂-še]-⸢e⸣-di kal₃ me-re-e-<ti> KASKAL-MEŠ den-ki-du₃ [...]

'taught all the pastures! The paths, o Enkidu, [...],

o 55

[lib-ka?]-⸢ka⸣ a-a i-qu!(TUR-)la GE₆ ur-ra [(...)]1

'may they mourn you, may they not fall silent by night or day [(...)]!

o 66

⸢lib⸣-ku-⸢na⸣-ka lu₂AB-MEŠ IRI DAGALeš UNUGki šu-pur-⸢ru⸣ [...]2

'May they mourn you, the elders of the extensive city of Uruk, the sheepfold! [...],

o 77

⸢um⸣-ma!(BA-)nu-um i-kar-rab EGIR-na lib₂-ki-ka [...]

'the crowd, giving blessings behind us! May it mourn you,1 [...]!

o 88

⸢lu⸣-u-⸢nam⸣-ba-a qer-bet a-ki-i AMA-ka lib₂-ki-ka giš[TAŠKARIN ...]

'May they lament, the meadows like your mother! May it mourn you, the boxwood tree, [...],

o 99

⸢ša⸣ ina bi-ri-su-nu ni-ḫi-tal-pu lib₂-ki-ka a-su ⸢bu⸣-[ṣu ...]3

'between which we crept! May it mourn you, the bear, hyena, [...],

o 1010

min₃-dan-nu lu-lim du-ma-mu UR.MAḪ AM-MEŠ a-a-li₃ [...]4

'cheetah, stag, (and) jackal, the lion, wild bulls, deer, [...],

o 1111

bu-ul nam-maš-e EDIN lib-ki-ka id₂u₂-la-a ⸢qad⸣-[diš-tu₂ ...]5

'the herds and animals of the open country! May it mourn you, the sacred River Ulay, [...]

o 1212

ni-tal-lak ina a-ḫa-šu₂ lib₂-ki-ka el-lat pu-ra-ti ⸢ša₂⸣ [...]6

'on its bank we always walked! May it mourn you, the holy Euphrates, which [...]

o 1313

A-MEŠ na-a-di lib₂-ki-ka lu₂GURUŠ-MEŠ IRI DAGALeš UNUG[ki ...]7

'as water from a water-skin! May he mourn you, the young men of the extensive city of Uruk, [...],

o 1414

⸢ša₂⸣ ME₃-MEŠ-na-ṭu-lu a-la <<IRI>> nu-na-er lib₂-ki-⸢ka⸣ lu₂[ENGAR ...]8

'who watched our battles, as we killed the Bull of Heaven! May he mourn you, the farmer [...],

o 1515

⸢ša₂⸣ a-la-ala DU₁₀.GA u₂-še-la MU-ka KI.MIN<(lib₂-ki-ka)> AB ⸢TI⸣ [...]9

who will extol your name with a sweet work song! Ditto (May he mourn you), ... [...]

o 1616

[x]-um-me-⸢e⸣ ma-ḫar-e u₂-še-la-a MU-ka KI.MIN<(lib₂-ki-ka)> ⸢lu₂⸣[NA.GADA ...]

'first [...] ... will extol your name! Ditto (May he mourn you), the herdsman [...],

o 1717

[x še]-⸢zib⸣-bi ḫe-e-mat u₂GI.NA ina pi-i-ka KI.MIN<(lib₂-ki-ka)> ⸢re?⸣-[eʾ-u₂ ...]10

'[...] set milk (and) ghee in your mouth! Ditto (May he mourn you), the shepherd [...],

o 1818

[x] ⸢iš⸣-ta-kan ina KI.TA-ka I₃.NUN.NA KI.MIN<(lib₂-ki-ka)> AB-⸢MEŠ⸣ [...]11

'[...] put ghee below you! Ditto (May he mourn you), the elders [...],

o 1919

[x] ⸢ku⸣-ru-u-nu iš-tak-kan <ina> pi-i-ka KI.MIN<(lib₂-ki-ka)> munus⸢ḫa⸣-[rim-tu₂ ...]

'[...] always put ale in your mouth! Ditto (May she mourn you), the prostitute [...],

o 2020

[x x] x I₃ tap-pa-šiš UGU-ka DU₁₀.GA lib₂-⸢ku⸣-[u₂ ...]12

'[...] ... anointed the crown of your head with sweet-scented oil! May they mourn [...],

o 2121

[x] ⸢e⸣-mu-tu₂ ša₂ DAM UN KUM IŠ KI KA x [...]13

'[...] the groom's marriage ceremony, who a wife ... [...]

o 2222

[x] ⸢ŠEŠ⸣-MEŠe lib₂-ku-u₂ UGU-ka GIM NIN-MEŠ-⸢ku⸣ [x x]14

'[...] brothers may they mourn over you! Like your sisters [...]

Reverse
r 1r 1

[uš]-šur pe-ra-tu₂-šun ⸢UGU⸣ [...]15

'their tresses be loosened on [...]!

r 22

KI.MIN<(lib₂-ku-u₂?)> ana den-ki-du₃ AMA-ka AD-ka ina na-me-šu-ma a-ba-[kak-ka ...]

'Ditto (May they mourn) for Enkidu, your mother and your father! On his steppe I will mourn you!

r 33

⸢ši⸣-ma-i-ni GURUŠ-MEŠ ši-ma-na-a-a-ši ši-ma-i-ni AB-MEŠ ⸢IRI⸣ [...]

'Listen to me, o young men, listen to me! Listen to me, o elders of the [...] city [...]!

r 44

ana-ku ana ib-ri-i den-ki-du₃ a-bak-ki ki-ma lalal-la-⸢ri⸣-[ti (...)]

'I shall mourn my friend Enkidu, like a professional female mourner [(...)]

r 55

a-nam-ba-a ⸢ṣar⸣-piš ḫa-ṣi-nu a-ḫa-a-a tu-ku-lat [...]

'I shall lament bitterly. The axe at my side, the trust of [...],

r 66

nam-ṣar ši-bi-i a-ri-te ša₂ pa-ni-ia mu-kil li-x x [...]

'the sword of my belt, the shield in front of me, the holder of ... [...],

r 77

lu-ba-ri-ši-na-ta-a ni-bi-iḫ lal-la-a-a [...]

'the garment of my festivals, the girdle of my delight: [...]

r 88

e-kim-ma-ni a-ia-ši ib-ri ku-dan-nu ṭar-du a-[ka-nu ...]

'it robbed me. O my friend, mule on the run, donkey [...],

r 99

nam-mar ša₂ EDIN den-ki-du₃ ku-dan-nu ṭar-du a-ka-nu ša₂ [...]

'panther of the open country, o Enkidu, mule on the run, donkey of [...]!

r 1010

ša i-ni-nu-ma ni-lu-u₂ KURu₂ ni-iṣ-ṣa-bat a-la <<IRI>> [...]16

'We who ... and went up the mountain, seized the Bull of Heaven [...],

r 1111

nu-ša₂-al-pi-te dḫum-ba-ba šar₄ gišTIR gišere-ni dan-⸢nu⸣ [...]

'destroyed Humbaba, mighty king of the Cedar Forest. [...]

r 1212

mi-na-a šit-tu₂ iṣ-bat-ka-ma ka-a-ši at-ta tur-ra-ma ⸢la⸣ [...]

'what sleep seized you? You, come back to me and [...] not [...].

r 1313

u šu-u₂ la i-na-ša₂-a SAG-MEŠ-šu₂ a-la-pa-te lib₃-ba-šu-⸢ma⸣ [...]

'But he (Enkidu), he is not lifting his head; I am feeling his heart but [...]

r 1414

mim-ma-ma ik-tu-ma ib-ri GIM E₂.GI₄.A pa-nu-uš ana-ku [...]

'at all.' He covered (his) friend's face like that of a bride, (saying:) 'I [...]

r 1515

a-su-ra UGU-šu₂ GIM UR.MAḪ ša₂ ina šu-ta-a-te mu-ra-[nu-ša₂ ...]

'I am circling over him. Like a lion whose cubs (are) in pits, [...]

r 1616

ina pa-ni-šu₂ u EGIR-šu₂ a-ba-qa-am a-ta-ba-ka qu-un-[nun-tu₄]

'in front of him and behind him. I am tearing out (and) discarding (my) curly

r 1717

⸢pe-er⸣-tu₄ i-na-as-saḫ!(ḪAR) i-na-da-a da-qa-a-te a-⸢šak⸣-[kiš ...]

'hair.' He was tearing off (and) throwing away (his) finery, like something taboo [...].

r 1818

[x x] ⸢d⸣GIŠ-gim₂-maš a-na KUR ri-gim ul-te-ṣi lu₂SIMUG!(MURUB₄) ⸢lu₂⸣[...]

[...] Gilgameš sent out a call to the land: 'Smith! [...] worker!

r 1919

[lu₂]⸢qur⸣-qur lu₂KU₃!(UD)DIM₂!(BAN) lu₂GAB₂.SAR e-pu-uš ib-⸢ri⸣ [...]17

'Coppersmith! Goldsmith! Jeweller! Make my friend [...]!'

r 2020

[...] ib-na-a ṣa-lam ib-ri-šu₂ ša₂ ib-ri-ia₂!(NIG₂) mi-⸢na⸣-[tu-ka? ...]

[...] he fashioned a statue of his friend: 'The limbs of my friend [...];

r 2121

[x x] x x ⸢i-ni⸣ ša₂ na₄ZA.GIN₃ GABA-ka ša₂ KU₃.SI₂₂ SU-⸢ka⸣ [...]18

'[...] ... the eyes are of lapis lazuli (and) your chest of gold, your body [...].'

1qu!(TUR) is only a tentative reading

2DAGAL{+eš} presumably represents rapeš for rapše; also obv. 13

3hi written over erasure

4erased DU between lim and du

5unusual form of ID₂

6ti written over erasure

7DAGAL{+eš} presumably represents rapeš for rapše; also obv. 6

8crasis for tāhāzna + iṭṭulū

9unusual form of la in a-la-ala; DU₁₀.GA written over erasure

10pi written over erasure

11TA written over erasure

12tap-pa-šiš probably for tupaššišu; ka DU₁₀.GA lib₂ written over erasure

13DAM $UN written over erasure

14unusual form of NIN

15[uš]-šur for uššurā

16KUR written over erasure

17UD.BAN written over erasure

18unusual form of KU₃.SI₂₂

1 in obverse 7-19 the verbal form lib₂-ki-ka can have singular or plural reference

Created by Frances Reynolds for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2008 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P273162
